heading: Requiring reports on medical malpractice payments
label: 11131 Requiring reports on medical malpractice payments

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Each entity (including an insurance company) which makes payment under a policy of insurance, self-insurance, or otherwise in settlement (or partial settlement) of, or in satisfaction of a judgment in, a medical malpractice action or claim shall report, in accordance with , information respecting the payment and circumstances thereof.section 11134 of this title
the name of any physician or licensed health care practitioner for whose benefit the payment is made,
the name (if known) of any hospital with which the physician or practitioner is affiliated or associated,
a description of the acts or omissions and injuries or illnesses upon which the action or claim was based, and
such other information as the Secretary determines is required for appropriate interpretation of information reported under this section.

children.heading: Sanctions for failure to report
children.label: c Sanctions for failure to report
children.text: Any entity that fails to report information on a payment required to be reported under this section shall be subject to a civil money penalty of not more than $10,000 for each such payment involved. Such penalty shall be imposed and collected in the same manner as civil money penalties under subsection (a) of are imposed and collected under that section.section 1320a–7a of this title

children.heading: Report on treatment of small payments
children.label: d Report on treatment of small payments
children.text: The Secretary shall study and report to Congress, not later than two years after , on whether information respecting small payments should continue to be required to be reported under subsection (a) and whether information respecting all claims made concerning a medical malpractice action should be required to be reported under such subsection.November 14, 1986
